meaning: vt.不再给予强调,使不重要
examples: One might think this would lead schools obsessed with winning to deemphasize academics.
To achieve conceptual integrity, deemphasize control mechanisms in favor of.
Sometimes the ads employ crafty timing or visual distraction to deemphasize the risks.
Expressions that represent strong emotions like astonishment or disgust, could emphasize or deemphasize different interactive elements on the TV.
When companies begin overemphasize risk management, and deemphasize opportunity management, the nauseous stench of status quo will begin wafting through the enterprise in epidemic proportion.
